title: Coryphantha clava
intro: Coryphantha clava, also known as the club cholla, is a type of cactus species belonging to the Echinocereus genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and fever, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha clava include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Coryphantha clava is classified within the family Cactaceae. This classification is based on its cactus-like appearance and characteristics. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. Within the genus Coryphantha, it is part of the section Echidno, which includes species with distinctive spines and flowers. Coryphantha clava is further distinguished by its unique morphology, but a more detailed classification may require further research.
